judgment.summary: A large, profitable company with strong analyst backing and significant cash flow, but facing modest growth and a substantial debt load.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat consensus; next earnings date 2026-07-28.
positives: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 9.5%.
Forward P/E of 18.43x is attractive relative to trailing P/E of 28.87x.
Strong free cash flow of $17.69B TTM.
Analyst consensus is 'buy' with a mean target of $390.35.
Dividend yield of 2.3% is supported by a 66.6% payout ratio.
negatives: Revenue growth of 2.0% YoY and earnings growth of 0.7% YoY are modest.
Quarterly earnings growth was negative at -0.2%.
Total debt of $77.92B significantly exceeds total cash of $31.23B.
Current ratio of 0.80 and quick ratio of 0.72 suggest potential short-term liquidity concerns.
Profit margin of 2.7% is relatively low.

news.paragraph: UnitedHealth Group (UNH) is facing mixed signals, with one report highlighting its managed care segment outperforming the economy while another notes a decline in share price following Berkshire Hathaway's exit from its stock. This divergence suggests underlying business strength is being weighed against potential shifts in major investor sentiment. The upcoming earnings report on July 28th will be a key event to watch for further clarity.

headlines.title: UnitedHealth Group (UNH) Gains as Managed Care Outgrows Economy
UnitedHealth (UNH) Shares Decline Following Berkshire Hathaway Exit, Reuters Reports
